This article details the [[Bradford Bulls]] rugby league football club's 2021 season. This is the Bulls' third consecutive season in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

==Season review==

===June 2020===

The preparation for the 2021 season started with the re-signing of experienced coach [[John Kear]] on a two-year extension which would keep him at the Bradford Bulls until the end of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls-secure-john-kear-new-deal-2872995|title=Bradford Bulls secure John Kear on new deal |work=Yorkshire Post |date=3 June 2020 |accessdate=24 November 2020}}</ref> Following this news, the Bulls announced that [[Super League]] veteran halfback [[Danny Brough]] would join the club on a 2-year deal from [[Wakefield Trinity]] linking up with John Kear for a third time.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18534785.bulls-sign-experienced-half-back-brough-two-year-deal/|title=Bulls to sign experienced half back Brough on two-year deal |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=23 June 2020 |accessdate=24 November 2020}}</ref>

===July 2020===
Vice captain [[Sam Hallas]] and homegrown fullback [[Brandon Pickersgill]] both signed one year deals to stay at the club for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18573893.vice-captain-hallas-agrees-one-year-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Vice-captain Hallas agrees one-year deal with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=10 July 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18604283.full-back-pickersgill-signs-new-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Full back Pickersgill signs new deal with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=24 July 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Winger [[Joe Brown (rugby league)|Joe Brown]] also signed an extension to stay with the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18619346.versatile-brown-signs-new-one-year-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Versatile Brown signs new one-year deal with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=31 July 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref>

===August 2020===
Due to the reserves being suspended until 2022 young halfback [[Cobi Green]] signed for [[Swinton Lions]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/swinton-sign-cobi-green-as-richard-lepori-extends-his-stay/|title=Swinton Sign Cobi Green |work=Love Rugby League |date=3 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Soon after Green's departure the Bulls announced the re-signing of second-row forwards [[Brad Gallagher]] and [[Ebon Scurr]], Gallagher signed a two-year extension whilst Scurr signed for one year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18634934.bradford-bulls-second-row-signs-deal-end-2022/|title=Bradford Bulls sign second row forward until the end of 2022 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18645397.prop-scurr-signs-new-one-year-deal-stay-bradford-bulls/|title=Prop Scurr signs new one-year deal to stay at Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> French prop forward [[Levy Nzoungou]] also signed a one-year extension to his contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18659715.nzoungou-signs-new-deal-bradford-bulls-next-season/|title=Nzoungou signs new deal with Bradford Bulls for next season |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=18 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Young hooker [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]] was the next player to sign an extension with the Bulls, signing on for another season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18663414.another-bulls-deal-done-hooker-doyle-signs-2021/|title=Another Bulls deal done as hooker Doyle signs for 2021 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> [[Luke Littlewood]] was the next player to leave the club due to no reserve grade for 2021, the young halfback signed for [[RFL League 1]] side [[Rochdale Hornets]], coached by former Bull [[Matt Calland]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18674354.bradford-bulls-youngster-littlewood-joins-rochdale-hornets/|title=Bradford Bulls youngster Littlewood joins Rochdale Hornets |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=25 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Bradford announced their second signing for the new season in the form of [[Huddersfield Giants]] second rower [[Aaron Murphy]] who signed a two-year deal with the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18682201.bradford-bulls-sign-huddersfields-murphy-two-year-deal/|title=Bradford Bulls sign Huddersfield's Murphy on two-year deal |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=28 August 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref>

===September 2020===
The month started with the news that winger [[Matty Dawson-Jones]] had taken up the option in his contract to stay an extra year at the Bulls after finishing last year's cancelled season as Bradford's top try scorer.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18688608.dawson-jones-takes-easy-decision-stay-bulls-2021/|title=Dawson-Jones takes "easy" decision to stay at Bulls in 2021 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=1 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> However last season's top point scorer [[Rowan Milnes]] was called back to his parent club [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] thus terminating his loan with the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18698412.rowan-milnes-back-hull-kr-bradford-bulls-loan/|title=Rowan Milnes back at Hull KR after Bradford Bulls loan |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Winger [[David Foggin-Johnston]] signed an extension to his contract to keep him at the Bulls until the end of 2021.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18699710.winger-foggin-johnston-signs-new-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Winger Foggin-Johnston signs new deal with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=5 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Second row forward [[Connor Farrell]] announced his retirement from the sport due to ongoing injuries. Following this news the Bulls signed [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]] second rower [[Brad England]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18707146.doncaster-second-rower-brad-england-joins-bulls-2021/|title=Doncaster second-rower Brad England joins Bulls for 2021 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Pack members [[Anthony England]] and [[George Flanagan]] both signed extensions with the club, prop forward England signed a two-year deal whilst hooker Flanagan signed a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18716244.prop-england-signs-new-deal-stay-bradford-bulls/|title=Prop England signs new deal to stay with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=12 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18723891.hooker-flanagan-remains-bulls-influence-off-pitch/|title=Hooker Flanagan remains Bulls influence on and off pitch |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=16 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Second rower [[Adam Rooks]] who was at the club on loan last season from Hull KR put pen to paper on a one-year permanent deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18750416.rooks-signs-one-year-deal-bulls/|title=Rooks signs one-year deal with Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Former Bulls centre [[Rhys Evans (rugby league)|Rhys Evans]] signed a two-year deal with the club following a short stint at [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18758283.evans-seals-two-year-deal-bulls-loan-spell/|title=Evans seals two-year deal with Bulls after loan spell |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=30 September 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref>

===October 2020=== 
There was positive news to start the month as the Bulls announced that they had retained reserve grade players [[Matthew Race]], [[Cameron Berry]] and [[Bradley Ho]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18782890.bradford-confirm-race-first-reserve-grade-player-staying/|title=Bradford confirm Race is first Reserve Grade player staying |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 October 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18793961.bradford-bulls-teenage-hooker-berry-signs-2021/|title=Bradford Bulls teenage hooker Berry signs up for 2021 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 October 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18800638.bradford-bulls-retain-front-row-prospect-reserves/|title=Bradford Bulls retain front-row prospect for reserves |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=16 October 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> The fourth reserve grade player to sign up for the new season was centre/second row [[Sam Smith (rugby league, born 1999)|Sam Smith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18812043.bradford-bulls-retain-prospect-sam-smith/|title=Bradford Bulls retain prospect Sam Smith |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 October 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> It was announced that homegrown prop forward [[Liam Kirk]] had signed for fellow Championship side [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] ending his 5-year stint at the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18828328.oldham-bring-bradford-bulls-forward-kirk/|title=Oldham bring in Bradford Bulls forward Kirk |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=28 October 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Another young reserve grade player in winger [[Murphy Smith (rugby league)|Murphy Smith]] signed up to the 2021 squad by signing a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18832932.bradford-bulls-retain-young-winger-2021/|title=Bradford Bulls retain young winger for 2021 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=30 October 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref>

===November 2020===
The Bulls revealed that the new kit supplier for the next two years would be [[Steeden]] after spending the last two seasons with [[Kappa (brand)|Kappa]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18840115.bradford-bulls-announce-two-year-kit-deal-steeden/|title=Bradford Bulls announce two-year kit deal with Steeden |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=2 November 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> The sixth new signing for the upcoming season was the former England Youth international wing/centre [[Reece Hamlett]] who signed a one-year deal with the club from [[Super League]] side [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18845098.bradford-bulls-bring-teenage-warrington-star/|title=Bradford Bulls bring in teenage Warrington star |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 November 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Another young reserve grade player in [[Billy Jowitt]] was retained by the club for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18854382.former-leeds-rhinos-prospect-stays-bradford-bulls/|title=Former Leeds Rhinos prospect stays on with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 November 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Following the decision to expel [[Toronto Wolfpack]] from the [[Super League]] the Bulls announced that they would be applying for the 12th place in the 2021 Super League season along with the likes of [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Toulouse Olympique]] and [[York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/18855010.bradford-throw-hat-super-league-ring-next-year/|title=Bradford throw hat into Super League ring for next year |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 November 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Chairman Mark Sawyer stated that a return to [[Odsal Stadium]] would be a key factor in applying for the Super League.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18857825.bulls-priority-odsal-return-bid-super-league/|title=Bulls priority is Odsal return as they bid for Super League |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 November 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Highly rated young [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] winger [[Elliot Wallis]] signed for Bradford on a year long loan deal in order to gain some first team experience.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/786/winger-wallis-jets-in-for-2021|title=WINGER WALLIS JETS IN FOR 2021 |work=Bradford Bulls |date=11 November 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref> Former prop forward [[Daniel Fleming (rugby league)|Dan Fleming]] who was at the club in 2015 and 2016 signed a one-year deal after a short stint at [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/794/fleming-pens-bulls-return|title=FLEMING PENS BULLS RETURN |work=Bradford Bulls |date=25 November 2020 |accessdate=25 November 2020}}</ref>

===December 2020===
Young homegrown second rower [[Evan Hodgson]] signed for [[RFL League 1]] side [[Newcastle Thunder]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18913895.evan-hodgson-leaves-bradford-bulls-newcastle-thunder/|title=Evan Hodgson leaves Bradford Bulls for Newcastle Thunder |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=2 December 2020 |accessdate=2 December 2020}}</ref> The Bulls application to [[Super League XXVI]] was not a success as [[Leigh Centurions]] were selected to be the 12th side for the 2021 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/18942722.bradford-miss-super-league-spot-leigh-get-nod/|title=Bradford miss out on Super League spot as Leigh get the nod |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 December 2020 |accessdate=22 December 2020}}</ref> Bradford also released their away kit which consists on Black and Red vertical stripes.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18955174.bradford-release-away-kit-2021-championship-season/|title=Bradford release away kit for 2021 Championship season |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=18 December 2020 |accessdate=22 December 2020}}</ref>

===January 2021===
There was no news in January.

===February 2021===
The Bulls announced that they would play local [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] side [[Keighley Cougars]] in a friendly in order to prepare for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19080612.bradford-keighley-play-friendly-challenge-cup/|title=Bradford and Keighley to play friendly before Challenge Cup |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=10 February 2021 |accessdate=13 February 2021}}</ref> Following this, the first round of the [[2021 Challenge Cup]] was drawn with the Bulls travelling to [[Featherstone Rovers]], the winners of the tie would then face either [[Halifax Panthers]] or [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the second round.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19084324.away-trip-fev-bradford-bulls-challenge-cup/|title=Away trip to Fev for Bradford Bulls in Challenge Cup |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 February 2021 |accessdate=13 February 2021}}</ref> Bradford announced their 2021 squad numbers with veteran new signing [[Danny Brough]] taking the number 6 shirt with [[Aaron Murphy]] wearing the number 12.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/860/bulls-reveal-2021-squad-numbers|title=BULLS REVEAL 2021 SQUAD NUMBERS |work=Bradford Bulls |date=12 February 2021 |accessdate=13 February 2021}}</ref> The Bulls revealed they would play one more friendly against [[Swinton Lions]] before the season begun.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19122031.bradford-host-swinton-friendly-next-weekend/|title=Bradford to host Swinton in friendly next weekend |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 February 2021 |accessdate=1 March 2021}}</ref>

===March 2021===
Bradford's 2021 campaign started with a 22–26 loss in the first pre-season friendly against [[Swinton Lions]], first half tries from [[Brandon Pickersgill]] and [[Ross Oakes]] with two conversions from [[Danny Brough]] gave the Bulls a 12–6 lead at half time however further tries from [[George Flanagan]] and [[Matty Dawson-Jones]] could not prevent a loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19141895.bradford-bulls-lose-first-pre-season-friendly-swinton/|title=Bradford Bulls lose first pre-season friendly to Swinton |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=6 March 2021 |accessdate=7 March 2021}}</ref> The Bulls announced the signing of 22-year-old wing/centre [[Jack Sanderson]] from [[Castleford Tigers]] on a one-year deal, Sanderson will wear the squad number 25 for the upcoming season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19152452.bradford-bring-exciting-castleford-talent/|title=Bradford bring in exciting former Castleford talent |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 March 2021 |accessdate=14 March 2021}}</ref> Bradford finished their pre-season off with an unconvincing 22–18 win over local rivals [[Keighley Cougars]], tries came from [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]], [[Danny Brough]], Ross Oakes and [[Anthony Walker (rugby league)|Anthony Walker]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19159133.bradford-stumble-victory-keighley-friendly/|title=Bradford stumble to victory over Keighley in friendly |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 March 2021 |accessdate=14 March 2021}}</ref> Trialist Anthony Walker earned himself a permanent contract with the Bulls after impressing during the pre-season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19172736.bradford-bring-former-super-league-star-permanent-deal/|title=Bradford bring former Super League star in on permanent deal |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=19 March 2021 |accessdate=24 March 2021}}</ref> Bradford's first competitive match in 373 days ended in a 41–16 defeat to [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the first round in the [[2021 Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19176810.report-bulls-suffer-challenge-cup-exit-playing-return/|title=REPORT: Bulls suffer Challenge Cup exit on playing return |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=22 March 2021 |accessdate=24 March 2021}}</ref> Following this defeat it was announced that the Bulls had signed former loanee [[Ben Evans (rugby league)|Ben Evans]] from [[Toulouse Olympique]] in order to bolster the options in the front row.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19180619.bradford-bring-welsh-international-evans-twin/|title=Bradford bring in Welsh international and Evans twin |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=24 March 2021 |accessdate=3 April 2021}}</ref>

===April 2021===
Prop forward [[Anthony England]] announced his retirement from the sport at the age of 34 due to ongoing injuries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19203923.bradford-forward-retires-due-injury-long-career/|title=Bradford forward retires due to injury after long career |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=1 April 2021 |accessdate=3 April 2021}}</ref> The opening game of the Championship season saw the Bulls on the end of a 50–12 defeat to the [[Sheffield Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19209767.bradford-crushed-sheffield-championship-opener/|title=SHEFFIELD 50 BULLS 12 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=5 April 2021 |accessdate=23 April 2021}}</ref> Super League side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] announced that [[Elliot Wallis]] would be returning to the club after a short loan spell with the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/932/wallis-returns-to-rovers|title=WALLIS RETURNS TO ROVERS |work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 April 2021 |accessdate=23 April 2021}}</ref> Bradford won their first game of the season in a hard-fought 27–26 victory over local rivals [[Halifax Panthers]], halfback [[Jordan Lilley]] kicked a late drop goal to ensure the win for the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19241836.report-battling-bulls-show-real-fight-edge-past-halifax/|title=BULLS 27 HALIFAX 26 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=19 April 2021 |accessdate=23 April 2021}}</ref> Young prop forward [[Bradley Ho]] was granted a season long loan move to [[RFL League 1]] side [[Coventry Bears]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19246793.bradford-bulls-loan-youngster-league-1-side-coventry/|title=Bradford Bulls loan youngster to League 1 side Coventry |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 April 2021 |accessdate=23 April 2021}}</ref> The Bulls made it back to back wins with a comprehensive 35–14 win over ground sharing side [[Dewsbury Rams]], halfback Brough made his 500th career appearance and marked the occasion with a try and a drop goal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19259659.bulls-brilliant-defence-beat-rivals-dewsbury-rams/|title=Bradford brilliant in defence to beat rivals Dewsbury Rams |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 April 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref>

===May 2021===
Bradford kept their winning streak going with a difficult trip to [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.]] as they came out 30–22 winners. Captain [[Steve Crossley]] led the way with two tries whilst [[Joe Brown (rugby league)|Joe Brown]], [[Brandon Pickersgill]] and Ebon Scurr all scoring as well.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19274603.match-report-white-bulls-make-three-wins-trot/|title=MATCH REPORT: All White for Bulls to make it three wins on trot |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=2 May 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref> The Bulls made it four wins in five with a tense 23–22 win over [[Swinton Lions]]. [[Danny Brough]], [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]], [[David Foggin-Johnston]] and Pickersgill all scored tries whilst [[Jordan Lilley]] secured the game winning drop goal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19290052.bradford-edge-swinton-tense-championship-battle/|title=Bradford edge out Swinton in tense Championship battle |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=10 May 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref> The scheduled game against [[Newcastle Thunder]] was rearranged to ensure that fans could attend as the government prepared to ease lockdown rules.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19292683.bradford-change-newcastle-date-due-sky-monday-trouble/|title=Bradford change Newcastle date due to Sky and Monday trouble |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 May 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref> The Bulls announced the permanent signing of former player [[Elliot Kear]] from [[Super League]] side [[Salford Red Devils]] as Kear wished to go part-time in order to train to be a fireman.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19304214.bradford-bulls-bring-elliot-kear-salford/|title=Bradford Bulls bring Elliot Kear in from Salford |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 May 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref> Long serving Football Manager Stuart Duffy announced his retirement after 23 years in the role after suffering a bout of ill health.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/967/stuart-duffy-retiring-from-role-with-club|title=STUART DUFFY RETIRING FROM ROLE WITH CLUB |work=Bradford Bulls |date=17 May 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref> Young centre [[Joe Burton (rugby league)|Joe Burton]] signed a {{frac|1|1|2}}-year professional deal with the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19314372.bradford-bulls-secure-future-young-back-end-2022/|title=Bradford Bulls secure future of young back until end of 2022 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=19 May 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref> Bradford announced the loan signing of [[Warrington Wolves]] forward [[Ellis Robson]] for a two-week period, meanwhile the Bulls also revealed they had signed [[Hull F.C.]] back [[Mitieli Vulikijapani]] on a two-week loan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19317720.warrington-forward-robson-signs-loan-bradford-bulls/|title=Bradford Bulls sign Robson and Vulikijapani on loan |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 May 2021 |accessdate=20 May 2021}}</ref> During this month the [[Rugby Football League]] announced that along with [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] the Bulls would not be granted an elite academy license after this season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls-shocked-rfl-academy-20648718|title=Bradford Bulls 'shocked' by RFL academy decision as club hits out |work=The Examiner |date=21 May 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> 300 travelling fans saw the Bulls beat [[London Broncos]] 33–8 in the capital with loan signing Robson the stand out player scoring two tries. Academy product [[Oliver Wilson (rugby league)|Oliver Wilson]] and fullback [[Ashton Golding]] both signed two-week loan deals with the Bulls from [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19330834.bradford-snap-talented-huddersfield-duo-loan/|title=Bradford snap up talented Huddersfield duo on loan |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 May 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> New centre [[Reece Hamlett]] signed with [[Swinton Lions]] on a loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://swintonlionsrlfc.co.uk/news/hamlett-joins-lions-on-loan/|title=HAMLETT JOINS LIONS ON LOAN |work=Swinton Lions |date=28 May 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> After spending a year and a half at [[Crown Flatt]], the Bulls made their long-awaited return to their home [[Odsal Stadium]] with a dominant 37–18 win against rivals [[York City Knights]], youngster [[Joe Brown (rugby league)|Joe Brown]] led the way with two tries but [[Danny Brough]]'s eight goals sealed the win.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19339160.bradford-bulls-thrash-york-odsal-return-goes-brilliantly/|title=Bradford Bulls thrash York as Odsal return goes brilliantly |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=31 May 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref>

===June 2021===
The upcoming match against [[Toulouse Olympique]] was cancelled due to travel restrictions from [[France]] regarding the ongoing [[COVID-19 pandemic]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19349990.coronavirus-rules-see-bradford-toulouse-clash-cancelled/|title=Coronavirus rules see Bradford and Toulouse clash cancelled |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 June 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> The [[Rugby Football League]] announced that the Bulls were granted a probationary elite license for their academy for a two-year period, renegading on their decision to take that license away.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19351247.bradford-bulls-told-can-continue-running-elite-academy/|title=Bradford Bulls told they can continue running elite academy |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 June 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> Bradford made it seven wins a row with a 31–12 victory over newly promoted [[Newcastle Thunder]], [[Steve Crossley]], [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]], [[Jordan Lilley]], [[Ellis Robson]] and [[Anthony Walker (rugby league)|Anthony Walker]] all scored tries for the Bulls whilst Lilley and [[Danny Brough]] kicked 5 goals between them.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19353536.report-bulls-seventh-heaven-newcastle-made-tough/|title=REPORT: Bulls in seventh heaven but Newcastle made it tough |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 June 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> However the Bulls suffered a serious blow as star halfback Brough was ruled out for 12–14 weeks with a bicep injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/19360415.injured-bradford-bulls-star-brough-bicep-operation/|title=Injured Bradford Bulls star Brough to have bicep operation |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 June 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> Loan signing [[Mitieli Vulikijapani]] was recalled by parent club [[Hull F.C.]] due to injury problems.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-hull-fc-squads-5502740|title=Mitieli Vulikijapani recalled as Brett Hodgson weighs up options for Hull FC clash with Castleford |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=8 June 2021 |accessdate=10 June 2021}}</ref> [[Huddersfield Giants]] also recalled [[Ashton Golding]] and [[Oliver Wilson (rugby league)|Oliver Wilson]] due to a number of positive Covid tests in their squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19362251.bulls-hit-loan-recalls-injury-covid-issues-fev/|title=Bulls hit by loan recalls, injury and Covid issues for Fev |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 June 2021 |accessdate=27 June 2021}}</ref> The Bulls winning streak came to an end as the injury hit Bulls were ruthlessly taken apart by unbeaten [[Featherstone Rovers]] in a 44–0 riot.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19369702.bradford-slump-featherstone-loss-championship-disaster/|title=Bradford slump to Featherstone loss in Championship disaster |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 June 2021 |accessdate=27 June 2021}}</ref> Young forward [[Ethan O'Hanlon]] was loaned out to League 1 side [[North Wales Crusaders]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://northwalescrusaders.com/2021/06/17/ethan-ohanlon-joins-crusaders-on-loan/|title=Ethan O’Hanlon joins Crusaders on loan |work=North Wales Crusaders |date=17 June 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref> [[Warrington Wolves]] recalled second rower [[Ellis Robson]] as their squad was depleted due to the England vs Combined All Stars match.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19387123.bradford-two-back-widnes-lose-couple/|title=Bradford to have two back for Widnes but lose a couple too |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 June 2021 |accessdate=27 June 2021}}</ref> With the injury to Brough, the Bulls announced the loan signing of former halfback [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]] on a 2-week deal from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19402152.bradford-get-keyes-back-loan-hull-kr/|title=Bradford get Keyes back on loan from Hull KR |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=27 June 2021 |accessdate=27 June 2021}}</ref> The Bulls suffered back to back defeats for the first time this season as they lost 25–12 to [[Widnes Vikings]], youngster Scurr and prop Walker scored the tries for Bradford.

===July 2021===

Bradford got back to winning ways with a hard-fought 30–16 win over 3rd placed [[Batley Bulldogs]] however they lost halfback [[Jordan Lilley]] to a rib injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19419093.bulls-waiting-verdict-lilley-goes-off-rib-injury/|title=Bulls waiting on verdict as Lilley goes off with rib injury |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 July 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref> After the victory over Batley the Bulls announced that the loan deal for scrum half [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]] was extended on a week by week basis.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1058/keyes-loan-extended|title=KEYES LOAN EXTENDED |work=Bradford Bulls |date=6 July 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref> The following week Bradford were ruthless as they dismantled [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] in a 54–22 thrashing, Keyes was the man of the match with a hat-trick of tries and nine goals in a 30-point haul.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19435346.bradford-bulls-secure-consecutive-victories-oldham-win/|title=Bradford Bulls secure consecutive victories after Oldham win |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 July 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref> Young halfback [[Matthew Race]] was sent on loan to [[North Wales Crusaders]] in order to gain some first team experience.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19443240.north-wales-bring-bradford-half-back-race-loan/|title=North Wales bring in Bradford half back Race on loan |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=15 July 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref> Once again [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] allowed halfback Keyes to stay with the Bulls for an extra month following some good performances.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19458134.bulls-extend-keyes-loan-deal-hull-kr-one-month/|title=Bulls extend Keyes loan deal from Hull KR by one month |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 July 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref> In what could be described as the game of the season so far the Bulls fell agonizingly short to the unbeaten [[Featherstone Rovers]] in a thrilling 30–36 loss which was only settled by a late [[Dane Chisholm]] interception.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19467764.keyes-bulls-left-sore-featherstone-heartbreak/|title=Keyes out as Bulls left sore over Featherstone heartbreak |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=25 July 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref> The Bulls announced that they had signed former loanee and current [[York City Knights]] player [[Jordan Baldwinson]] one a one-week loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19481839.bradford-bring-york-star-loan-halifax-clash/|title=Bradford bring York star in on loan for Halifax clash |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=30 July 2021 |accessdate=31 July 2021}}</ref>
